title: Pelecyphora robbinsiorum
taxonomy: Pelecyphora robbinsiorum is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico and is also known as the 'Robbins' pincushion cactus'. As a member of the Cactaceae family, it is classified under the subfamily Cactoideae. The genus Pelecyphora itself is part of the tribe Pachycereeae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the cactus family.

intro: Pelecyphora robbinsiorum, commonly known as the "Cholla de Robbins," is a species of cactus native to Mexico, belonging to the family Cactaceae. The Cholla de Robbins is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to the reduction of oxidative stress and the alleviation of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and betalains, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the Cholla de Robbins is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues.
